objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 gradebook authorization assessment with client-controlled role escalation.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real external target is contacted.
Student, teacher, and grade records are intentionally fake.

phases.objective: Map the gradebook write path and role inputs.
actions: Load the synthetic gradebook update behavior
Identify whether role and teacher context are server-owned or client-controlled
successCriteria: Gradebook endpoint evidence is recorded with a durable evidence ID
noSubagents: authorization scope and student-data handling decisions stay with the primary operator

phases.objective: Validate gradebook write escalation through mass assignment.
actions: Replay a student-scoped request that supplies teacher role fields
Record the mutated grade and the expected server-side authorization check
successCriteria: A report-ready finding cites both the request evidence and the missing control evidence
noSubagents: final severity and disclosure wording stay with the primary operator

evidence.title: Synthetic student grade update request
evidence.summary: A student-scoped request changed a grade after supplying teacher role fields in the request body.

evidence.title: Synthetic gradebook server-owned role gap
evidence.summary: The synthetic route trusts request-body role and teacher fields instead of deriving authorization from the authenticated server-side session.
evidence.content: Expected control: ignore client-supplied role, teacherId, and canEdit fields; derive gradebook write permission from the authenticated session and enforce course ownership before mutating grades.

findings.title: Student-controlled gradebook write escalation
findings.description: The synthetic gradebook endpoint accepts role and teacher context from the request body, allowing a student-scoped request to update a protected grade record.
findings.impact: A student account could alter academic records without teacher approval, undermining grade integrity and district reporting workflows.
findings.remediation: Make role and course ownership server-owned, reject client-supplied privilege fields, and require an authenticated teacher session with explicit course ownership before grade mutations.
